data.title: Bond Market Will Scrutinize a September Fed Cut — Market Talk
data.description:
0845 ET - In his speech at the Jackson Hole conference on Friday, Fed Chair Jerome Powell began to outline the case for an interest-rate cut in September. There is still more labor-market and inflation data to come before that meeting, though, and price increases have been accelerating as tariffs mount. The rate cut case that Powell laid out rests on a presumption that these price increases will be a one-time phenomenon. Yet the bond market might take some convincing, writes Ed Yardeni, the economist who coined the phrase "bond vigilantes" in the 1980s. If the Fed cuts in September, "Powell will have to explain that the committee has judged that tariffs are having a transitory impact on keeping inflation around 3.0%, but it should soon be falling to 2.0%," Yardeni writes. "The Bond Vigilantes might not be persuaded." (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

0804 ET - The furniture-specific tariff that President Trump said on Friday he's planning would result in higher furniture prices, particularly for low-income consumers, says Stifel analyst W. Andrew Carter. "We believe boosting U.S. job growth is difficult requiring a long timeline for consistent investment, at best, and would likely further accelerate furniture inflation disproportionately impacting lower-end consumers," Carter says. Lower-priced furniture has narrower product margins, Carter explains, meaning furniture tariffs would likely be regressive. Inflation data has already suggested a significant increase of furniture prices faster than the rest of the economy, which would be worsened by additional tariffs, Carter says. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0758 ET - Gold futures inch lower, but remain elevated as markets continue to digest U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's Friday speech at the Jackson Hole symposium. Futures are down 0.1% at $3,416 a troy ounce, but are up 1.1% on week. The U.S. dollar and bond yields fell and gold gained Friday after Powell opened the door to an interest rate cut in September, ING analysts say in a note. Powell had pointed to increasing labor market risks, despite persistent inflation concerns. Traders are now increasing bets on rate cuts and gold could be poised for another fresh record high in the short-term, ING adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0652 ET - The U.S. dollar and U.S. Treasury yields rise, reversing some of Friday's sharp falls triggered by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's remarks at Jackson Hole. Powell opened the door to an interest-rate cut in September. However, investors are cautious before upcoming data. Thursday's second estimate of second-quarter GDP and Friday's July PCE price index will be closely watched, says Naga's Frank Walbaum in a note. "Stronger growth or sticky inflation could reinforce expectations of a slower easing path and lend temporary support to the dollar and yields, while softer readings may keep pressure on the currency and yields." The DXY dollar index rises 0.3% to 97.968. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 1.3 basis points to 4.270%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0649 ET - Bitcoin falls to a six-week low of $111,096, according to LSEG. The crypto currency has dropped back since hitting an all-time high of $124,480 on August 14. Monday's fall comes as European equity markets turn lower and reverse some of their gains on Friday. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell opened the door to a likely interest-rate cut in September at a speech at Jackson Hole on Friday, boosting risk appetite. However, analysts say investors are reassessing Friday's enthusiasm as a new week gets underway. "The river ahead appears far from being ripple-free," FP Markets' Aaron Hill says in a note, adding that Powell also cautioned that the Fed would proceed carefully. Bitcoin is last down 1.25% at $111,375. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0616 ET - The cost of insuring euro-denominated investment-grade bonds against default falls to a one-week low after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell opened the door to a rate cut in September in a speech on Friday. "Markets ended last week in a buoyant mood as a dovish tilt by Powell at Jackson Hole left investors increasingly confident on upcoming Fed easing," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. The iTraxx Europe Main index of euro investment-grade CDS falls to 51 basis points on Monday, from 52bps late Friday, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index of euro junk-bond credit default swaps fell sharply to 261bps late Friday and remains at that level on Monday. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0555 ET - German business appears to be putting a lot of faith in government spending to come, ING economist Carsten Brzeski writes in a note to clients. The closely-watched Ifo index showed a continued rise in sentiment among German companies this month, dispelling some of last week's gloomy figures showing a sharper second-quarter contraction in the German economy than initially estimated. But the source of this optimism is unclear given U.S. trade tariffs and a generally unpromising outlook for German exporters, Brzeski says. Hopes must largely rest on the major new fiscal investment in defense and infrastructure promised by Chancellor Friedrich Merz. "[The] index shows the remarkable optimism of German businesses in the healing nature of fiscal stimulus for the economy," he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0523 ET - Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech on Friday provides a "clear yield curve steepening impulse" to U.S. Treasurys, ING's Padhraic Garvey says in a note. Short-dated yields will likely drop after Powell's comments fueled expectations of interest-rate cuts, while long-dated yields could stay elevated due to inflation concerns, says Garvey, who is head of global debt and rates strategy. "The front end now has Chair Powell on its side, and yields there should stay down." U.S. money markets price an 84% chance of a September rate cut, LSEG data show. The two-year Treasury yield is last at 3.711%, while the 30-year yield trades at 4.892%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0518 ET - The futures market is now pricing in two 25bps Federal Reserve rate cuts by December, leaving gold well-supported, Kudotrade analyst Konstantinos Chrysikos says in a note. Fed Chair Jerome Powell is signalling risks to the labor market are increasing amid elevated inflation and suggesting that the Fed may need to adjust its restrictive stance, which weighs on U.S. Treasury yields and benefits the precious metal, says Chrysikos. Upcoming U.S. GDP growth and personal-consumption-expenditures data will be key in determining the Fed's policy path, he adds. Softer inflation and weaker growth figures could further support gold. Spot gold falls 0.2% to $3,363.52/oz. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

0427 ET - German Bunds and the euro barely react to a better-than-expected German Ifo business-climate index. The Ifo index for August rose to 89.0 from 88.6 in July, beating the consensus forecast of 88.3 in the Wall Street Journal's poll of analysts. Sentiment in Europe's largest economy has picked up steadily this year, although Ifo President Clemens Fuest said the German's recovery "remains weak." The 10-year Bund yield trades at 2.756%, up 3.6 basis points on the day, versus 2.755% before the data. The euro trades at $1.1703, up marginally from $1.1697 beforehand but still down 0.1% on the day. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0420 ET - The euro could rise to $1.20 by the end of 2025 if the U.S. Federal Reserve opts for a series of interest-rate cuts, ING's Padhraic Garvey says in a note. Fed Chair Jerome Powell opened the door to a rate cut in September at his speech at Jackson Hole on Friday when he said that "shifting risks may warrant adjusting policy." ING forecasts the euro at $1.17 by the end of the current quarter but expects prospects of further rate cuts will lift it further. The euro is last down 0.1% on the day at $1.1703, having hit a near four-week peak of $1.1742 on Friday. U.S. money markets price an 84% chance of a rate cut next month and fully price in two 25 basis-point reductions by December, LSEG data show. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0357 ET - The two-year Treasury note has recorded one of its best year-to-date performances outside of a recession since 2000, and without a single interest-rate cut from the Federal Reserve, says Payden & Rygel in a note. Two-year Treasury yields started the year at their highest level since 2008, giving investors attractive income, it says. Then yields came down due to the historic increase in U.S. trade tariffs. This caused markets to expect weaker growth. Labor-market data have deteriorated while inflation has remained subdued, Payden & Rygel says. The two-year Treasury yield last trades at 3.713%, up 2.5 basis points, according to Tradeweb. It started 2025 at 4.248%. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.description:
Talphera Inc :

data.title: Coya rises after US FDA nod for ALS therapy mid-stage trial
data.description:
** Shares of Coya Therapeutics rise 7.9% to $7.40 premarket

** U.S. FDA gives its go-ahead to the drugmaker to study its drug, COYA 302, as a potential treatment of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) in a mid-stage study

** The acceptance of the Investigational New Drug application by the FDA triggers a $4.2 million milestone payment to Coya from Indian drugmaker Dr Reddy's Laboratories

** ALS, also known as Lou Gehrig's disease, is a rare neurological disease that affects the motor neurons in the brain and spinal cord, leading to muscle weakness, paralysis, and eventually, death

** Up to last close, stock up 19.7% YTD

data.title: Thoma Bravo Acquires Verint to Join Forces with Calabrio to Create an AI-Driven Customer Experience Powerhouse
data.description:
MINNEAPOLIS & SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 25, 2025--

Thoma Bravo, a leading software investment firm, announced today that it has entered into a definitive agreement to purchase Verint Systems, Inc. ("Verint") in an all-cash transaction reflecting an enterprise value of $2 billion for the company. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory approvals, and is expected to close before the end of Verint's current fiscal year, in early 2026. More details on the transaction can be found via Verint's Investor Relations page and in its regulatory filings.

Following the close of the transaction, Calabrio and Verint will be combined into one company and will be a leading provider of Customer Experience (CX) Automation Solutions to the $50B+ market in which they serve. Together, they will offer an expansive portfolio to advance the critical priorities of CX organizations across the size and complexity spectrum. The combination will create more opportunities for companies to quickly achieve business outcomes in their interactions with customers. Calabrio is fully committed to maintaining and investing in the products that support its installed base and customers' workflows.

"Together Calabrio and Verint will bring a powerful set of products to accelerate a shared vision: delivering an AI-powered, open CX-platform to customers who are focused on driving strong business outcomes in their operations. As a combined company we are well positioned to lead the industry forward," said Dave Rhodes, Calabrio CEO.

Mike Hoffmann, a Partner at Thoma Bravo added: "We have been active in the CX space for many years and are excited to bring these two companies together to lead more innovation and growth in the category. Calabrio and Verint both have powerful product portfolios and go-to-market strategies that cover the needs of a wide spectrum of the market. Together, the combined company will have the industry's broadest CX platform, enabling brands of all sizes to drive transformative, AI-driven outcomes."

About Thoma Bravo

Thoma Bravo is one of the largest software-focused investors in the world, with approximately $184 billion in assets under management as of March 31, 2025. Through its private equity, growth equity and credit strategies, the firm invests in growth-oriented, innovative companies operating in the software and technology sectors. Leveraging Thoma Bravo's deep sector knowledge and strategic and operational expertise, the firm collaborates with its portfolio companies to implement operating best practices and drive growth initiatives. Over the past 20+ years, the firm has acquired or invested in approximately 535 companies representing approximately $275 billion in enterprise value (including control and non-control investments). The firm has offices in Chicago, Dallas, London, Miami, New York and San Francisco. For more information, visit Thoma Bravo's website at thomabravo.com.

About Calabrio

Calabrio is a trusted ally to leading brands. The digital foundation of a customer-centric contact center, the Calabrio ONE workforce performance suite helps enrich and understand human interactions, delivering business outcomes by optimizing every customer interaction. We maximize agent performance, exceed customer expectations, and boost workforce efficiency using connected data, AI-fueled analytics, automated workforce management, and personalized coaching. Only Calabrio ONE unites workforce optimization (WFO), agent engagement, and business intelligence solutions into a cloud-native, fully integrated suite that adapts to your business. Calabrio, Calabrio ONE, and the Calabrio logo are registered trademarks or trademarks of Calabrio, Inc. All other trademarks mentioned in this document are the property of their respective owners. Calabrio operates in Canada under Calabrio Canada, Ltd., based in British Columbia.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250824564235/en/

CONTACT:

For further information contact

data.title: Political and General News Events from August 25
data.description:
For other diaries, please see:

Political and General News

Top Economic Events

Emerging Markets Economic Events

Government Debt Auctions

U.S. Federal Reserve

Today in Washington

--------------------------------------------------------------------

This diary is filed daily. ** Indicates new events

--------------------------------------------------------------------

MONDAY, AUGUST 25

** JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia - Turkey's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan attends the Extraordinary Session of the Council of Foreign Ministers of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) in Jeddah.

** KUALA LUMPUR – Sultan of Brunei Hassanal Bolkiah undertakes a working visit to Malaysia (to August 28).

** WARSAW - Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney visits Warsaw, holds bilateral meeting with Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk, meets Polish president Karol Nawrocki. Polish and Canadian ministers of defence will also hold meetings with focus on security, and strengthening ties between Poland and Canada - 0900 GMT

** KYIV - German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil, who also serves as Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s deputy, visits Kyiv - 1000 GMT

** ZAGREB - German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul visits Zagreb. He holds joint presser with Croatian Foreign Minister Gordan Grlic-Radman. - 1000 GMT

** SINGAPORE - President of the Republic of Ghana, John Dramani Mahama, arrives in Singapore for a three-day state visit (to Aug 28)

** ALPBACH, Austria - President of the Republic of Kosovo begins a visit to Austria to attend the European Forum Alpbach (to Aug 27).

** RIYADH - Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar, begins a visit to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (to Aug 26)

STOCKHOLM - Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Hellenic Republic, Giorgos Gerapetritis, and Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs of Greece, Ioannis Loverdos, visit Sweden. (final day)

EDINBURGH, Scotland - Edinburgh Fringe Festival 2025 (final day)

ASTANA/TASHKENT - Japan Foreign Minister Takeshi Iwaya will visit Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan and will meet with Kazakh Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Murat Nurtleu and Uzbek Foreign Minister Bakhtiyor Saidov respectively to boost bilateral ties with Japan. (to August 28).

NEW DELHI - Fiji Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka to visit India and meet President Droupadi Murmu, Prime Minister Narendra Modi (to August 26).

BRASILIA - Nigerian President Bola Tinubu will leave for Brasilia, the capital of the Federative Republic of Brazil, on a two-day state visit (final day)

URUGUAY - Uruguay's Independence Day.

PARIS – 81st anniversary of the liberation of Paris from Nazi occupation in the Second World War.

- - - - - - - - -

TUESDAY, AUGUST 26

** ASTANA - King of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, Abdullah II ibn Al Hussein, begins visit to the Republic of Kazakhstan (to Aug 27).

** RIGA - Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina to hold bilateral talks with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney in Riga. Latvia is host of Canadian-led NATO Multinational Brigade.

BERLIN - German Chancellor Friedrich Merz receives Canadian Prime Minister, Mark Carney and at the chancellery in Berlin - 0800 GMT.

BERLIN - German Chancellor Friedrich Merz receives Belgian Prime Minister Bart De Wever at the chancellery in Berlin - 1200 GMT.

- - - - - - - - -

WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 27

** BERLIN - President of the Republic of Bulgaria, Rumen Radev, begins a two-day working visit to Germany (to Aug 28).

CHISINAU – President of France Emmanuel Macron, Chancellor of Germany Friedrich Merz and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk visit Moldova.

EGYPT - Egyptian Senate election.

VENICE, Italy – 82nd Venice International Film Festival (to September 6).

BUSAN, Republic of Korea – APEC Energy Ministerial Meeting (EMM) (To Aug. 28).

- - - - - - - - -

THURSDAY, AUGUST 28

** BRAZIL - Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva welcomes Panama's President José Raul Mulino in Brasilia

** TOKYO - Britain’s Defence Minister John Healey holds bilateral talks with his Japanese counterpart Gen Nakatani in Tokyo, followed by a joint press conference.

BERLIN - Germany's Defence Minister Boris Pistorius receives Spanish counterpart Margarita Robles with military honours in Berlin - 1230 GMT.

BEIJING – Nepal Prime Minister Khadga Prasad Sharma Oli to visit China for SCO summit.

data.title: Verint, Thoma Bravo Transaction Is Not Subject to a Financing Condition >VRNT
U.S. Economy Slows as September Rate-Cut Chances Grow — Chicago Fed
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

The U.S. economy is losing pace, according to a monthly index released Monday, adding to the likelihood that policymakers will move to lower interest rates next month.

The Chicago Fed's National Activity Index inched down to minus 0.19 in July from a downwardly revised minus 0.18 in June. A reading below zero suggests growth was slower than the long-term average.

Employment-related indicators remained negative on the index, a fresh sign of fragility in the U.S. labor market. That weakness is one of the main motives driving a push toward a reduction in interest rates at next month's Fed policy meeting. The central bank's chair, Jerome Powell, last week noted the underlying lackluster trend in the country's jobs market. That "may warrant adjusting our policy stance," Powell said in a speech at the Jackson Hole economics meeting in Wyoming on Friday.

Most investors now expect the Fed to lower its key borrowing rate by a quarter point in September, according to LSEG Refinitiv data. This would be the first rate-cut since last year.

Two of the other categories gauged by the Chicago Fed's index also were negative in July, while one, personal consumption and housing, made a neutral contribution to the overall reading, highlighting the broad lack of momentum in the economy.

The index's three-month moving average rose to minus 0.18 from minus 0.26 in June, while the diffusion index--which captures how much the monthly change is spread among the individual indicators over three months--rose to minus 0.31 from minus 0.40 in June.

Periods of economic expansion have historically been associated with values of the CFNAI diffusion index above minus 0.35.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: SentinelOne Cut to Neutral From Buy by BTIG
Zhihu Inc expected to post a loss of 13 fen a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Zhihu Inc , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 27 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Beijing-based company is expected to report a 20.4% decrease in revenue to CNY743.611 million from CNY933.81 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Zhihu Inc is for a loss of 13 fen per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the online services peer group is also "Buy".​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Zhihu Inc is $5.90, about 19% above its last closing price of $4.78
